findings.notes: Type-level dependency on better-sqlite3; Turso/libSQL has different type surface (e.g. no Statement generic, different Database interface).
Module-level better-sqlite3 Database handle stored as singleton; Turso client is async and has different connection lifecycle (HTTP/WebSocket vs in-process).
Init accepts better-sqlite3 Database instance; Turso libSQL client has incompatible API (async .execute() vs sync .prepare().all()).

findings.signature: SELECT name FROM sqlite_master WHERE type='table' AND name='memory_fts'
findings.notes: Checks sqlite_master for memory_fts FTS5 virtual table; Turso/libSQL lacks FTS5 — query will return empty or error.
Delegates to FTS5 bm25() ranking function; Turso/libSQL has no FTS5 — this entire search channel fails.

findings.signature: db.prepare(`SELECT id, spec_folder, importance_tier FROM memory_index WHERE id IN (...)`)
findings.notes: Synchronous better-sqlite3 .prepare().all() pattern; Turso requires async .execute() and returns different row shape.

findings.signature: db.prepare(`SELECT m.* FROM memory_index m JOIN active_memory_projection ...`)
findings.notes: Synchronous .prepare().all() with positional params (? placeholders); Turso uses named params or positional but async API.
Produces Buffer for sqlite-vec vec0 virtual table storage; sqlite-vec extension not available in Turso/libSQL.
Type dependency on better-sqlite3 BetterSqlite3 namespace.

findings.signature: database.prepare(`SELECT payload_hash, response_payload FROM memory_idempotency_receipts WHERE receipt_key = ?`).get()
findings.notes: Synchronous .prepare().get() pattern; Turso requires async .execute() with different result envelope.

findings.signature: database.prepare(`INSERT INTO memory_idempotency_receipts ... ON CONFLICT ... DO UPDATE SET ...`).run()
findings.notes: Synchronous .prepare().run() with ON CONFLICT upsert; Turso async API returns different result (rowsAffected vs changes).

findings.signature: database.prepare(`SELECT updated_at, last_dedup_checked_at FROM memory_index WHERE id = ?`).get()
findings.notes: Synchronous better-sqlite3 .prepare().get(); Turso async .execute() with different row access pattern.

findings.signature: database.prepare(`UPDATE memory_index SET near_duplicate_of = ?, last_dedup_checked_at = datetime('now') WHERE id = ?`).run()
findings.notes: Synchronous .prepare().run() with datetime('now') — SQLite-specific function; Turso supports it but async API differs.
Uses .prepare().get() to test handle liveness; Turso async client makes this probe async, changing the assertion contract.

findings.signature: export function planStatediff(desiredRows, priorRows, options): StatediffAction[]
findings.notes: No direct SQLite/driver coupling; pure data-diff logic. Migration-neutral.

findings.signature: export function resolveCanonicalDbDir(dir: string, workspaceRoot?: string): string
findings.notes: No direct SQLite/driver coupling; filesystem path resolution only. Migration-neutral.
